parsed.artifact_title: Boundaries of Claim in the Event-Loop Append-Only Comparison

claims.text: Artifact non-inferiority uses a declared 0.10 mean quality margin.
Scheduler observability is reported separately from artifact quality.
Provider or transport failure makes the run inconclusive rather than proving model inferiority.

parsed.conclusion: The append-only comparison framework can assert artifact non-inferiority only within the declared 0.10 quality margin. It cannot fuse scheduler observability into a composite quality judgment, because observability is reported on a structurally separate track. It cannot interpret provider or transport failures as evidence of model inferiority — such runs are inconclusive and represent gaps in the comparison record. The framework therefore yields a bounded, conservative signal: it can confirm that an artifact falls within an acceptable margin of the baseline, but it cannot certify superiority, unify disparate evaluation dimensions, or redeem failed runs.
declared_losses: The 0.10 quality margin is declared, not learned from data, so non-inferiority claims are bounded by that threshold rather than empirically derived.
Scheduler observability is reported separately from artifact quality; the framework does not support claims that couple the two into a single composite judgment.
Provider or transport failures render runs inconclusive — they cannot be interpreted as evidence of model inferiority, only as gaps in the comparison record.
parsed.recommended_action: Limit claims to non-inferiority inside the declared 0.10 margin. Treat observability as a parallel, non-composited report. Exclude all provider-failed runs from inferiority interpretation and flag them as missing data. Do not extrapolate beyond what the append-only record supports.

carried_state.work_plan: Synthesize from the three evidence records: (1) the declared 0.10 margin constrains what non-inferiority can claim, (2) observability is structurally separate from quality, and (3) provider failures yield inconclusive runs, not inferiority proofs. The synthesis note will articulate the boundary between what the event-loop append-only comparison can assert and what it must reserve as uncertain or outside scope.
